members.name: AddDatabase
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBDatabaseResource> DocumentDBBuilderExtensions.AddDatabase(this IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> builder, string name, string? databaseName = null)

summary.text: Adds a DocumentDB database to the application model.

remarks.text: The database resource inherits the parent server's connection string and appends the database name. Services should reference the database resource (not the server) via
. This resource includes a built-in health check. When this resource is referenced as a dependency using the
extension method then the dependent resource will wait until the DocumentDB database responds to ping.

databaseName.text: The name of the database. If not provided, this defaults to the same value as

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

summary.text: Adds a DocumentDB resource to the application model. A container is used for local development.

remarks.text: This resource includes a built-in health check. When this resource is referenced as a dependency using the
extension method then the dependent resource will wait until the DocumentDB server responds to ping. This version of the package defaults to the

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

members.name: AllowInsecureTls
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> DocumentDBBuilderExtensions.AllowInsecureTls(this IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> builder, bool allowInsecureTls = true)

summary.text: Allows insecure TLS connections by adding
tlsInsecure=true
to the connection string. This is enabled by default so the .NET MongoDB driver can connect to the self-signed certificate used by the DocumentDB Local container. Call
AllowInsecureTls(false)
to require valid certificates.

remarks.text: because the .NET MongoDB driver does not fully honor

allowInsecureTls.text: Whether to allow insecure TLS. Defaults to

members.name: UseTls
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> DocumentDBBuilderExtensions.UseTls(this IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> builder, bool useTls = true)

summary.text: Enables TLS for the DocumentDB connection string. TLS is enabled by default because the DocumentDB Local container requires TLS connections. Call
UseTls(false)
to disable TLS if connecting to a non-TLS endpoint.

useTls.text: Whether to enable TLS. Defaults to

members.name: WithDataBindMount
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> DocumentDBBuilderExtensions.WithDataBindMount(this IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> builder, string source, bool isReadOnly = false)

summary.text: Adds a bind mount for the data folder to a DocumentDB container resource.

remarks.text: for most cases. Bind mounts are useful when you need direct access to the data files on the host filesystem. The bare DocumentDB container defaults
. This helper mounts the directory at
to the same value so DocumentDB writes to the mounted directory.

isReadOnly.text: A flag that indicates if this is a read-only mount.

source.text: The source directory on the host to mount into the container.

members.name: WithDataVolume
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> DocumentDBBuilderExtensions.WithDataVolume(this IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> builder, string? name = null, bool isReadOnly = false, string? targetPath = null)

summary.text: Adds a named volume for the data folder to a DocumentDB container resource.

remarks.text: Without a volume, all data is stored inside the container and lost when it stops. The bare DocumentDB container defaults
. This helper mounts the volume at
to the same value so DocumentDB writes to the mounted directory.

isReadOnly.text: A flag that indicates if this is a read-only volume.

name.text: The name of the volume. Defaults to an auto-generated name based on the application and resource names.

targetPath.text: The target path inside the container. Defaults to /data to match the container default when this helper is used.

summary.text: Enables or disables DocumentDB Local telemetry.

members.name: WithTlsCertificate
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> DocumentDBBuilderExtensions.WithTlsCertificate(this IResourceBuilder<DocumentDBServerResource> builder, string certPath, string keyPath)

summary.text: Mounts a custom TLS certificate and key into the DocumentDB Local container.

remarks.text: The certificate and key files are mounted at distinct container paths so that they do not collide even if their host file names are identical.

certPath.text: The certificate file to mount into the container.

keyPath.text: The private key file to mount into the container.

summary.text: Represents the supported DocumentDB Local container log levels.

enumMembers.name: Info
enumMembers.description: Logs informational messages, warnings, and errors. This is the default DocumentDB Local log level.

enumMembers.name: Debug
enumMembers.description: Logs debug-level diagnostic messages and everything above.

enumMembers.name: Trace
enumMembers.description: Logs the most verbose trace-level diagnostic messages and everything above.

children.text: workflow when a new upstream version is published. Existing members and their explicit numeric values must never be renamed, removed, or reordered, as that would be a binary breaking change for callers compiled against earlier versions of this package. Members that should no longer be used are marked
to pin a specific version. Use
to enumerate all known versions or read the latest version known to this build.

All DocumentDB versions known to this build of the package, sorted ascending by numeric semantic version. The order of this list is part of the contract:
All[All.Count - 1]
is always the newest known version.

remarks.text: The returned instance is a read-only wrapper that cannot be downcast to a mutable array. This protects
(which reads from this list) from being silently changed at runtime.
